question: I went through his work; it was <u>gibberish</u>
section: From the words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word as it is used in the sentence
solution: Gibberish is a meaningless speech or writing. Option A is the correct answer

question: You need to __________your points
section: From the words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that best completes each of the following sentences.
solution: Option C, 'Expatiate' is the appropriate spelling

question: I am quite <u>outspoken</u> but my brother is __________
section: From the list of words or group of words lettered A to D, choose the one that is most nearest opposite in meaning to the underlined word or group of words and that will, at the same time correctly fill in the gap in the sentence.
solution: Outspoken means speaking freely or openly. However, Taciturn means reserved in speech.

question: How old was Aliya in the Novel?
section: This question is based on Bolaji Abdullahi's Sweet Sixteen
solution: The text made references of years before Aliya turned 16, however, Aliya is sixteen (16). This is evident in page 1, sentence 3.

question: he economist argued that the apparently <u>inconsequential</u> rise in the price of petrol had a _________ effect on the cost of living
section: From the list of words or group of words lettered A to D, choose the one that is most nearest opposite in meaning to the underlined word or group of words and that will, at the same time correctly fill in the gap in the sentence.
solution: inconsequential simply means having no consequence. However, dangerous means the ability to cause harm. This makes option  C correct.

question: After Ade had listened to some advice, his <u>rigid</u> ideas became more ______________
section: From the list of words or group of words lettered A to D, choose the one that is most nearest opposite in meaning to the underlined word or group of words and that will, at the same time correctly fill in the gap in the sentence.
solution: Rigid' is the opposite of the word ''Flexible'. Rigid implies unbending as opposed to Flexible which means 'capable of being bent'

question: Adoringly, Aliya was referred to as ________
section: This question is based on Bolaji Abdullahi's Sweet Sixteen
solution:
Mr Bello calls his daughter, Aliya, 'First lady'

See page 2

question: Although Chide's manners are quite <u>refined</u> , his colleagues are rather ___________
section: From the list of words or group of words lettered A to D, choose the one that is most nearest opposite in meaning to the underlined word or group of words and that will, at the same time correctly fill in the gap in the sentence.
solution: Refined implies to purify or to be free from impurities. Option B; crude, is a direct opposite to the word 'refine'' which means being in a natural state.

question: The Major demanded an <u>unalloyed</u> and not a __________ loyalty from his soldiers
section: From the list of words or group of words lettered A to D, choose the one that is most nearest opposite in meaning to the underlined word or group of words and that will, at the same time correctly fill in the gap in the sentence.
solution: unalloyed means not mixed, complete or unadulterated.
